user_query:
Read the following inputs and produce a multi-category private web research package:

1) input/search_plan.json
   - This file defines a topic, default language, default time_range, per-item queries, categories, and per-item limits. Apply the plan exactly: use the default language/time_range unless an item overrides. Respect each item's limit as a maximum number of results kept.

2) input/exclusions.txt
   - Line-separated domain substrings to exclude (case-insensitive). Any URL whose hostname contains one of these substrings must be excluded from the final deduplicated list and from the Sources section.

3) input/brief.md
   - Context and tone for the final report. Use it to frame the Executive Summary and prioritize findings.

Your tasks:
A) Execute the searches per plan using your available private metasearch capability. Do not rely on absolute or machine-specific paths. For each plan item, save raw search output to a separate JSON file under output/raw/ using this strict filename rule:
   - Filename: output/raw/<slug>__<category>.json
   - Slug rules: lowercase, spaces -> '-', remove characters other than [a-z0-9-]. Example: "Enterprise self hosted tools" -> enterprise-self-hosted-tools

   Each raw file must be a JSON object with keys: query (string), category (string), language (string), time_range (string), results (array). Each results[] item must at least include title (string) and url (string). Keep at most the per-item limit results.

B) Build a cross-file index at output/index.json with this structure:
   {
     "topic": string (from input/brief.md or input/search_plan.json if provided there),
     "generated_at": ISO-8601 timestamp string,
     "items": [
       {
         "query": string,
         "category": string,
         "language": string,
         "time_range": string,
         "file": string (relative path to the raw JSON you wrote),
         "result_count": number (must match the results length in that raw JSON)
       }, ...
     ]
   }

C) Deduplicate by URL across all raw files, then filter out any URLs whose hostname contains any substring listed in input/exclusions.txt. Save the final unique, filtered list as a JSON array of strings to output/deduped_urls.json. Ensure all entries are unique and relative path references are not included—only full URLs.

D) Write a structured report to output/report.md that:
   - Includes these section headings exactly once each: "# Executive Summary", "## Methodology", "## Findings", "## Sources", "## Appendix: Deduplicated URLs"
   - States the applied search parameters in the body: "Time range: <value>" and "Language: <value>"
   - States "Total unique URLs: <N>" where N equals the length of output/deduped_urls.json
   - Summarizes key findings tailored to the context in input/brief.md
   - Uses numbered citations in the Sources section (e.g., [1], [2], ...) and lists at least three source URLs that are actually present in your raw files and not excluded. Link them inline next to the numbers.
   - Includes an appendix listing the deduplicated, filtered URLs (you may truncate to the first 20 if there are many, but make clear if you truncated)

Notes & constraints:
- Use only relative workspace paths (input/, output/). Do not write anywhere else.
- If any search yields zero results or a request fails, still create the corresponding raw file with an empty results array and proceed with the rest; reflect counts accurately.
- Ensure filenames and JSON structures match the specifications exactly so downstream checks can validate your work.
